section.heading: Transportation of a probationer
section.chapeau: A court, after imposing a sentence of probation, may direct a United States marshal to furnish the probationer with—

subsection.content: transportation to the place to which he is required to proceed as a condition of his probation; and
money, not to exceed such amount as the Attorney General may prescribe, for subsistence expenses while traveling to his destination.

note.heading: Effective Date
note.p: Section effective Nov. 1, 1987, and applicable only to offenses committed after the taking effect of this section, see section 235(a)(1) of Pub. L. 98–473, set out as a note under section 3551 of this title.
